_comment: Regression baselines for the Maelardalen (MRTC) WCET benchmark suite on MSP430.
Consumed by tests/regression_test.py. Each benchmark is BUILT and RUN through llta
(loop bounds come from the Clang LoopBoundPlugin via the #pragma loop_bound(...)
annotations in tests/srcMaelardalen/*.c). The recorded outcome is the contract:
expected: <int>  -> analysis produced this WCET; must reproduce exactly.
expected: null   -> a valid run that produces NO WCET (build-fail / crash /
analyze-fail). 'status'+'note' document why. A crash is a
valid test run: re-running must still produce no WCET (GREEN).
Refresh a value here when it legitimately changes; flip null<->int when behaviour
changes. status vocabulary: build-fail | crash | analyze-fail | unsound.

bsort100.note: Uses the source #pragma loop_bound values (100,99,99) now that JSON is preferred over the looser SCEV maxBTC; was 327478 when one loop took the maxBTC bound of 100 instead of the asserted 99.

adpcm.note: Upstream LLVM MSP430 backend bug (not LLTA): opt rewrites my_sin's +/-2*PI range-reduction into llvm.smax.i16(x,-6282); MSP430 EmitCMP/LowerSELECT_CC does getConstant(C->getSExtValue()+1) = -6281 = 0xFFFF...E757, which LLVM 20's strict APInt(16,val,unsigned) ctor asserts on (isUIntN failed) instead of truncating. Dormant before the LLVM 20 upgrade. Not fixing (would require patching vendored LLVM).

compress.note: Now bounded. The real unbounded cycles were not the goto-probe interlock (all of compress()'s source loops -- probe 111, while 50 -- were already bounded with backedges) but backend-synthesized MSP430 multi-bit shift loops (`add rX,rX` / `clrc;rrc rX` single-block self-loops) in compress()/output(), which have no source pragma and no IR loop. RTTarget::getImplicitLoopBound now recognizes the shift-loop idiom and bounds it by the shifted operand's bit width (a sound upper bound). UNSOUND: body-less __mspabi_* integer mul/div/mod/shift helpers costed as 0.

crc.note: Context-sensitive call/return matching constraints bound the previously-unbounded inter-procedural cycle (a callee reached from multiple sites had its return edges merged to every landing). Sound: no body-less calls.

dijkstra.note: Linker R_MSP430X_ABS16 reloc truncated: two 100x100 long arrays exceed the 16-bit data address space.

duff.note: Now bounded and SOUND. Duff's device lowers to a multi-entry loop (a switch jump-table branches into the middle of the do-while body), so MachineLoopInfo recognizes no loop and the bound 6 never reached LoopBoundMap. The loop-bound pass now finds the single backedge (LBB0_9->LBB0_2) via a DFS retreating-edge scan, matches the do-while pragma against the loop body, bounds the header, and records the backedge for ProgramGraph to flag -- the existing per-header IPET row then bounds the whole multi-entry SCC (the maximize picks the worst entry). No body-less calls, so sound.

edn.note: Now bounded. Every source loop was already bounded; the residual unbounded cycles were backend-synthesized MSP430 shift loops (`rra rX; sub.b #1,rN; jne` single-block self-loops) in jpegdct, which have no source pragma and no IR loop. RTTarget::getImplicitLoopBound recognizes the shift-loop idiom and bounds it by the operand bit width (sound upper bound; a 32-bit shift's two-word rra+rrc sums to 32). UNSOUND: body-less __mspabi_mpyi/mpyl/sral and memcpy costed as 0.

expint.note: JSON pragma bound is now preferred over the loose SCEV maxBTC (32766) that previously yielded ~88.5M.

fft1.note: opt instcombine fixpoint-verifier abort fixed via instcombine<no-verify-fixpoint> in the Makefile (matches the default -O2 pipeline, which disables fixpoint verification). UNSOUND: double soft-float routines (__mspabi_addd/mpyd/divd/...) are body-less and costed as 0.

fir.note: RAM overflow: in_data[701] + out_data[720] longs exceed MSP430 RAM.

janne_complex.note: Outer while(a<30) bound (11) is now applied: after loop-rotation+ISel its machine-loop header maps to IR block 'entry' (LoopInfo reports no IR loop), so the clean-IR/SCEV path dropped it -> unbounded. The MachineLoopBoundAgregatorPass machine-block pragma fallback now matches the bound against the loop's own machine blocks (inner still 9). UNSOUND: b*3 lowers to a body-less __mspabi_mpyi (i16 multiply), costed as 0.

lms.note: Bonus: this soft-float kernel shared the multi-call unboundedness; the call/return matching constraints now bound it. UNSOUND: soft-float __mspabi_* routines are body-less and costed as 0.

ludcmp.note: FIXUP binary-grep fixed (grep -a); now fails at a later build step (no .elf produced).

matmult.note: Now bounded by the per-call-site call/return matching constraints (Initialize() is called twice; its single return block was merged to both landing pads, forming an unbounded inter-procedural cycle). UNSOUND: RandomInteger uses soft integer mul/div (__mspabi_*), body-less in the IR and costed as 0.

minver.note: Bonus: soft-float kernel that shared the multi-call unboundedness; bounded now by the call/return matching constraints. UNSOUND: soft-float __mspabi_* routines body-less, costed as 0.

ndes.note: Bounded by the call/return matching constraints (helpers reached from many sites -- one 9x -- no longer form unbounded cycles). UNSOUND: body-less __mspabi_* helpers costed as 0.

prime.note: Bounded by the call/return matching constraints (main->prime->divides; divides reached from multiple sites). UNSOUND: body-less __mspabi_* integer mul/div/mod helpers costed as 0.

qurt.note: Bonus: soft-float kernel that shared the multi-call unboundedness; bounded now by the call/return matching constraints. UNSOUND: soft-float __mspabi_* routines body-less, costed as 0.

recursion.note: Links now (In defined). No loops to bound -- the analyzer bounds loops, not the fib() recursion -> Failed to compute WCET.

sqrt.note: Added a self-contained main(); the kernel implements its own fabs/sqrtfcn (no external libm), so the WCET is sound.

statemate.note: Was non-deterministic on a start-function ref-count tie; tie now broken (starts from main) -> deterministic. Self-contained, all 302 graph nodes reachable.

ud.note: RAM overflow: long a[50][50] (~10KB) overflows FR5994 RAM.

whet.note: Deterministically produces 30161778, now emitted with an explicit *** UNSOUND *** block listing the body-less calls it under-counts (libm sin/cos/atan/log/exp/sqrt and the __mspabi_* soft-float routines, whose bodies are absent from the analyzed IR). Pinned as the contract for that marked under-approximation; costing those routines is impractical (MSP430X code LLVM can't decode + a statically unresolvable indirect call in the soft-float tree -- see docs/LibraryCallCosting.md), so the honest UNSOUND mark stands.
